description: Concrete verify trigger and workflow eval definitions through Milestone 4, including the must-run validation boundary pack and one pinned cryptoli real-repo workflow case.

trigger_evals.goal: Exercise verify's trigger boundary on concrete plans and claims.

cases.prompt: Review plans/2026-04-20-eval-harness-fixture-plan.md against AGENTS.md and evals/fixtures/eval-harness-roadmap.md and tell me whether the plan passes.
cases.why: There is a concrete plan target to judge.

cases.prompt: I do not know whether the next move should be consult, plan, or execute. Recommend the safest next move.
cases.why: Clarification and recommendation belong to consult.

cases.prompt: Implement the next milestone from this plan file and run the smallest meaningful checks.

trigger_evals.goal: Protect verify's ownership of concrete diff and claim review.

cases.prompt: Review this concrete implementation slice for bugs, regressions, missing specs/tests sync, and blocked checks. Return findings first and a verdict.
cases.why: A concrete implementation target exists and needs adversarial judgment.

cases.prompt: Create or update a plan file for this task so a fresh session can resume it.
cases.why: Durable task-state maintenance belongs to plan.

cases.prompt: Fix the failing test and stop once the suite passes.
cases.why: Implementation belongs to execute or tests, not verify.

workflow_evals.goal: Judge plan-review quality against repo truth and explicit sync obligations.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the verify skill materially improves adversarial plan review over an unguided judgment.

repo_state: AGENTS.md requires findings-first review and treats missing required specs/tests sync as fail.
evals/fixtures/eval-harness-roadmap.md defines the evaluation-harness follow-up scope.
Inspection may be enough if no mechanical check is needed.

assertions: Returns findings first and names the verification target.
Checks scope, sequencing, blockers, and explicit specs/tests follow-through against repo truth.
When the target is an explicit plan path, writes the verdict and any blocking follow-up back into that same plan file.
Runs or skips only the smallest meaningful checks and explains skipped checks honestly.
Ends with a verdict and remaining gaps.
review_questions: Does the review stay adversarial instead of defending the plan?
Are any findings grounded in specific file references or command evidence?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge implementation-review quality for a concrete slice with changed code, docs, and tests.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the verify skill materially improves concrete implementation review and missing-sync detection.

scenario.user_request: Review a concrete implementation slice that changed src/execute/SKILL.md, README.md, and one test file.
repo_state: The claimed outcome is that execute's behavior changed and the tests were synced.
The review must separate unrelated baseline noise from problems introduced by the slice.
Missing required specs or tests sync should be treated as fail when the obligation is clear.

assertions: Returns findings first with file references or command evidence.
Checks correctness, regressions, and missing required specs/tests sync.
Separates unrelated baseline failures from newly introduced problems.
Ends with a clear verdict and remaining gaps.
review_questions: Does the review catch material regressions instead of stopping at a surface summary?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether verify can review a concrete cryptoli repo-truth and test-truth sync against the pinned fixture and return findings first.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether verify improves findings-first review quality on a real monorepo fixture.

scenario.user_request: Review a concrete cryptoli sync slice that changed AGENTS.md, specs/README.md, and specs/testing-strategy.md. Return findings first and a verdict.
repo_state: The pinned fixture is a three-app pnpm workspace with root repo-truth docs and layered backend plus frontend/admin tests.
The candidate slice claims to sync repo truth and testing guidance after the monorepo grew.
Missing required specs or tests sync should fail when the obligation is clear from the fixture and the claimed change.

assertions: Returns findings first with file references or command evidence.
Checks whether the three-app workspace, repo-truth surface, and testing layers are represented accurately against the pinned fixture.
Flags missing required repo-truth or test-truth sync instead of rubber-stamping the slice.
Ends with a clear verdict and any blocked checks.
review_questions: Does the review stay adversarial and grounded in the actual fixture rather than generic doc-review language?
